text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-hook-form repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: a subscription created via `form.subscribe({ formState: { ... }, callback })` receives a payload that includes the `name` of the field involved in the event that triggered it. If you call `clearErrors('firstName')` and then, later, trigger an unrelated form-state update that has no field name of its own (for example submitting the form via `handleSubmit`), the subscriber's callback fires with `name` still set to `'firstName'` — a stale value left over from the earlier `clearErrors` call — instead of `undefined`. In other words, the per-event metadata (`name`/`type`) from one notification is being persisted into form state and bleeding into later, unrelated notifications. Fix the library source so that per-event metadata is not persisted across updates and each subscriber notification reports only the name (if any) of the event that actually triggered it. Fix the library source (under `src/`); do not just add workarounds in test files.

Environment notes: dependencies are already installed (via `npm install -g pnpm@10.34.5 && CYPRESS_INSTALL_BINARY=0 pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts`). Run tests with `node_modules/.bin/jest --config ./scripts/jest/jest.config.js src/__tests__/useForm/subscribe.test.tsx`.

Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-hook-form repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: a subscription created via `form.subscribe({ formState: { ... }, callback })` receives a payload that includes the `name` of the field involved in the event that triggered it. If you call `clearErrors('firstName')` and then, later, trigger an unrelated form-state update that has no field name of its own (for example submitting the form via `handleSubmit`), the subscriber's callback fires with `name` still set to `'firstName'` — a stale value left over from the earlier `clearErrors` call — instead of `undefined`. In other words, the per-event metadata (`name`/`type`) from one notification is being persisted into form state and bleeding into later, unrelated notifications. Fix the library source so that per-event metadata is not persisted across updates and each subscriber notification reports only the name (if any) of the event that actually triggered it. Fix the library source (under `src/`); do not just add workarounds in test files.

Environment notes: dependencies are already installed (via `npm install -g pnpm@10.34.5 && CYPRESS_INSTALL_BINARY=0 pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts`). Run tests with `node_modules/.bin/jest --config ./scripts/jest/jest.config.js src/__tests__/useForm/subscribe.test.tsx`.

I’ll trace how `subscribe` builds notification payloads and how `clearErrors` and submit updates populate form-state metadata, then make the smallest source-level fix. I’ll run the focused subscription tests and the required build afterward.

input.cmd: pwd && rg -n "subscribe|clearErrors|\bname\b|\btype\b" src/logic src/useForm.ts src/__tests__/useForm/subscribe.test.tsx | head -240

text: Continue the task until all targets are completed. [context: sender_id=human|channel=$cli|chat_id=local]
`rg` is unavailable in this checkout, so I’m switching to the repository’s available shell tools and continuing the same source trace.

input.cmd: find src -type f | sort | head -80 && grep -RInE "subscribe|clearErrors" src/logic src/useForm.ts src/__tests__/useForm/subscribe.test.tsx | head -240

text: Continue the task until all targets are completed. [context: sender_id=human|channel=$cli|chat_id=local]

The relevant implementation is concentrated in `src/logic/createFormControl.ts`, with existing subscription coverage in the requested test file. I’m reading the notification construction and nearby mutators now to identify where event fields are being merged into persistent state.

input.cmd: sed -n '560,660p' src/logic/createFormControl.ts; sed -n '1210,1410p' src/logic/createFormControl.ts; sed -n '1,380p' src/__tests__/useForm/subscribe.test.tsx

text: Continue the task until all targets are completed. [context: sender_id=human|channel=$cli|chat_id=local]

input.cmd: grep -n "_subjects\.state\.next" src/logic/createFormControl.ts; grep -n "_setFormState\|const _formState\|_formState =\|formState:" src/logic/createFormControl.ts | head -100; grep -RIn "last changed field\|clearErrors.*name\|name.*clearErrors" src/__tests__ | head -100

text: Continue the task until all targets are completed. [context: sender_id=human|channel=$cli|chat_id=local]

The stale value can arise because subscription callbacks merge the latest event payload over `_formState`, while `_setFormState` appears to copy payload metadata into `_formState`. I’m checking every `_setFormState` call and event emitter to distinguish intended persistent form-state updates from transient `name`/`type` fields before editing.

input.cmd: sed -n '120,175p' src/logic/createFormControl.ts; sed -n '200,350p' src/logic/createFormControl.ts; sed -n '380,550p' src/logic/createFormControl.ts; sed -n '800,1110p' src/logic/createFormControl.ts; sed -n '1160,1210p' src/logic/createFormControl.ts; sed -n '1395,1435p' src/logic/createFormControl.ts; sed -n '1835,1880p' src/logic/createFormControl.ts; grep -RIn "type:" src/logic/createFormControl.ts | head -100

text: Continue the task until all targets are completed. [context: sender_id=human|channel=$cli|chat_id=local]

The persistence point is confirmed: `shouldRenderFormState` invokes `_setFormState` with event payloads, and `_setFormState` currently spreads every key into `_formState`, including transient `name` and `type`. I’ll update that helper to strip only those event metadata keys while preserving all actual form-state fields and existing event payload behavior.
Continue the task until all targets are completed. [context: sender_id=human|channel=$cli|chat_id=local]
